title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Audit  Configuration Changes

input_row.title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Audit  Configuration Changes
input_row.description: Google Cloud Platform (GCP) services write audit log entries to the Admin Activity and Data Access logs to help answer the questions of, "who did what, where, and when?" within GCP projects. Cloud audit logging records information includes the identity of the API caller, the time of the API call, the source IP address of the API caller, the request parameters, and the response elements returned by GCP services. Cloud audit logging provides a history of GCP API calls for an account, including API calls made via the console, SDKs, command-line tools, and other GCP services.
input_row.rationale: Admin activity and data access logs produced by cloud audit logging enable security analysis, resource change tracking, and compliance auditing. Configuring the metric filter and alerts for audit configuration changes ensures the recommended state of audit configuration is maintained so that all activities in the project are audit-able at any point in time.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Ensure the prescribed log metric is present: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. In the User-defined Metrics section, ensure that at least one metric is present with the filter text: protoPayload.methodName="SetIamPolicy" AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.auditConfigDeltas:* Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 3. Go to Alerting by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerting. 4. Under the Policies section, ensure that at least one alert policy exists for the log metric above. Clicking on the policy should show that it is configured with a condition. For example, Violates when: Any logging.googleapis.com/user/ stream is above a threshold of 0 for greater than zero(0) seconds, means that the alert will trigger for any new owner change. Verify that the chosen alerting thresholds make sense for the user's organization. 5. Ensure that appropriate notifications channels have been set up. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure that the prescribed log metric is present: 1. List the log metrics: gcloud beta logging metrics list --format json 2. Ensure that the output contains at least one metric with the filter set to: protoPayload.methodName="SetIamPolicy" AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.auditConfigDeltas:* 3. Note the value of the property metricDescriptor.type for the identified metric, in the format logging.googleapis.com/user/. Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 4. List the alerting policies: gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json 5. Ensure that the output contains at least one alert policy where: • conditions.conditionThreshold.filter is set to metric.type=\"logging.googleapis.com/user/\" • AND enabled is set to true
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Create the prescribed log metric: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics and click "CREATE METRIC". 2. Click the down arrow symbol on the Filter Bar at the rightmost corner and select Convert to Advanced Filter. 3. Clear any text and add: protoPayload.methodName="SetIamPolicy" AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.auditConfigDeltas:* 4. Click Submit Filter. Display logs appear based on the filter text entered by the user. 5. In the Metric Editor menu on the right, fill out the name field. Set Units to 1 (default) and Type to Counter. This will ensure that the log metric counts the number of log entries matching the user's advanced logs query. 6. Click Create Metric. Create a prescribed Alert Policy: 1. Identify the new metric the user just created, under the section User-defined Metrics at https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. Click the 3-dot icon in the rightmost column for the new metric and select Create alert from Metric. A new page opens. 3. Fill out the alert policy configuration and click Save. Choose the alerting threshold and configuration that makes sense for the organization. For example, a threshold of zero(0) for the most recent value will ensure that a notification is triggered for every owner change in the project: Set `Aggregator` to `Count` Set `Configuration`: - Condition: above - Threshold: 0 - For: most recent value 4. Configure the desired notifications channels in the section Notifications. 5. Name the policy and click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Create a prescribed Log Metric: • Use the command: gcloud beta logging metrics create • Reference for command usage: https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/beta/logging/metrics/create Create prescribed Alert Policy • Use the command: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create • Reference for command usage: https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/alpha/monitoring/policies/create
input_row.impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_user_defined_metrics_audit_config_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
1. Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) to verify the existence of logs-based metrics and alert policies:
   - **List log metrics:** Execute the command:
     ```
     gcloud beta logging metrics list --format json
     ```
     Check if at least one log metric with the filter:
     ```
     protoPayload.methodName="SetIamPolicy" AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.auditConfigDeltas:*
     ```
     exists. Validate that the metricDescriptor.type property has the format `logging.googleapis.com/user/`.
   
   - **List alerting policies:** Execute the command:
     ```
     gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json
     ```
     Ensure that there is at least one alert policy where:
     - `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` is set to `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/"`.
     - `enabled` is set to `true`.
   
2. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - For the logs-based metrics:
     - Expected: At least one metric matching the specified filter.
     - Pass if this condition is met; fail otherwise.
   - For the alert policies:
     - Expected: At least one policy that meets the criteria described.
     - Pass if this condition is met; fail otherwise. 

By implementing this automated approach, organizations can maintain consistent oversight of their audit log configurations and ensure compliance with CIS benchmarks.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as "Automated" is correct. The tasks described can be effectively executed using Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands, which are suitable for automation. The use of `gcloud beta logging metrics list` and `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` commands demonstrates that the checks can be performed programmatically without manual intervention.
- The approach leverages GCP APIs through the Google Cloud SDK, which is appropriate for this type of compliance check.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The `gcloud beta logging metrics list` command is valid for listing log-based metrics, and the filter specified is appropriate for identifying metrics related to audit configuration changes.
- The `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` command is also valid for listing alerting policies. However, the use of `alpha` commands may indicate that these features are not yet fully stable or generally available, which could impact reliability.
- The program name `gcp_logging_user_defined_metrics_audit_config_changes` is descriptive and follows a reasonable naming convention. However, it could be slightly more specific by including terms like 'alert_policies' to fully capture the scope of the check.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- Consider verifying the availability of the `beta` and `alpha` commands in the target environment, as these may not be enabled by default or could be subject to change.
- It would be beneficial to include a step to ensure that the log metric and alert policy are not only present but also correctly configured to trigger alerts when audit configuration changes occur.
- The program name could be improved to: `gcp_logging_audit_config_changes_alert_policies_check` to better reflect the dual focus on both log metrics and alert policies.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and uses appropriate GCP commands. The technical details align with GCP's capabilities for managing and auditing logs and alert policies.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- While the overall approach is sound, I recommend modifying the program name for greater clarity and ensuring that the use of `beta` and `alpha` commands is appropriate for the environment. Additionally, consider adding checks for the correct configuration of the log metrics and alert policies to ensure they are functioning as intended.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the audit approach can be automated using Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. The tasks involve checking the existence of logs-based metrics and alert policies, which can be effectively executed programmatically. The use of `gcloud beta logging metrics list` and `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` commands allows for the necessary checks without manual intervention, making automation feasible and efficient.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
gcp_logging_user_defined_metrics_alert_policies_audit_config_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Logging and Monitoring
- API/CLI Method: 
  - `gcloud beta logging metrics list --format json`
  - `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json`
- Properties to Check:
  - For log metrics: Check for the presence of a metric with the filter `protoPayload.methodName="SetIamPolicy" AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.auditConfigDeltas:*`.
  - For alert policies: Ensure there is at least one alert policy where `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` is set to `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/"` and `enabled` is `true`.
- Pass Criteria:
  - Log metrics: At least one metric matching the specified filter exists.
  - Alert policies: At least one policy meets the criteria described.
- Fail Criteria:
  - Log metrics: No metric matching the specified filter exists.
  - Alert policies: No policy meets the criteria described.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported an automated approach. The decision was made based on the technical feasibility of using GCP APIs to perform the necessary checks.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Google_Cloud_Platform_Foundation_Benchmark_v4_0_0
